issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, and no other visual cue (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` (rotateY/rotateX) on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion override disabling this movement, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1159: `.hero-image img { transition: none; }`  
  (Add a matching rule inside `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }` if you prefer to keep the original transition.)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies a 3‑D transform, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1165: `.hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }`  
  (Add the same rule inside the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block.)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button moves upward with `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1190: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; }`  
  (Place the rule inside `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`.)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies a combined `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` on hover, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, breaking WCAG 2.3.3 for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1300: `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }`  
  (Add this inside the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block.)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and toggling a CSS‑only scroll‑lock, but it never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element. When the drawer is closed, screen‑reader users cannot know it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
function close(announceToSR) {
  …
  _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');   // add this line
  …
}
```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer visible state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function makes the drawer visible by adding CSS classes, but it never removes `aria‑hidden` or sets it to `"false"` on the drawer element. Assistive technologies therefore still treat the drawer as hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
function open(announceToSR) {
  …
  _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');   // add this line
  …
}
```

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hidden via hidden attribute but missing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` the panel is hidden with `panel.setAttribute('hidden','')` but the corresponding `aria‑hidden="true"` is never applied. This leaves the panel in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel shown without clearing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the panel is shown by removing the `hidden` attribute, yet any existing `aria‑hidden="true"` remains, causing the panel to stay hidden to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close()` function hides the answer panel with `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden','')` but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`. The panel stays in the accessibility tree while collapsed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel shown without clearing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but leaves any existing `aria‑hidden` attribute untouched, so the panel remains hidden to screen readers even when visible.

File‑upload field does not announce added files via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a file is added (`_addFile`), the UI updates the file list but no live‑region announcement is made for each addition, leaving users of assistive technology unaware that a file was queued.
issuesFound.fix:
after creating the row in `_addFile` add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(`${file.name} added to upload queue.`);
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates visual state and ARIA attributes, but no dedicated live‑region message is emitted. Users who cannot see the visual change receive no feedback that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `_openInstance` and after setting it to `'false'` in `_closeInstance`, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(inst.trigger.textContent.trim() + ' menu opened'); // in open
liveRegion.announce(inst.trigger.textContent.trim() + ' menu closed'); // in close
```

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button lacks `aria-pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s play/pause button (`_playBtn`) toggles automatic rotation but never updates `aria-pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot know the current state.

issuesFound.title: Search result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, the script updates a status element (`_statusEl`) with the number of results, but the element is not an `aria-live` region, so users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`mobileNav.close()`), the script removes CSS classes but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, leaving it perceivable to AT even though it is visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not update `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The header is hidden on scroll (`header:hidden` class) but the element’s `aria-hidden` state is never toggled, so screen‑reader users may still navigate to a hidden header.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack `aria-hidden` updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened (`_openInstance`) the script removes the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="false"`. Conversely, when closed (`_closeInstance`) it only adds the `hidden` attribute without setting `aria-hidden="true"`. AT therefore cannot reliably determine visibility.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu outside‑click close does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking outside a dropdown closes it, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware that the menu has been dismissed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing `aria-hidden` when initially hidden
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, dropdown panels are given the `hidden` attribute but not `aria-hidden="true"`, so AT may still expose them.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing `aria-hidden` when shown after init
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened for the first time, the script removes `hidden` but does not clear `aria-hidden`, leaving the panel announced as hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing `aria-hidden` when closed after being opened
issuesFound.explanation: After a panel has been opened, closing it only adds `hidden` without restoring `aria-hidden="true"`, so the element may still be perceivable to AT.

issuesFound.title: Empty‑state visibility changes are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The function **_updateEmptyState** shows or hides the empty‑state element when no products match, but it does not provide any live‑region feedback. Users of assistive technologies are left unaware that the result set is empty.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert a live region (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “No products found” when the empty state is shown, clearing it when hidden. ---

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message changes are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The function **_updateSavingsMessage** updates the text of the savings call‑out when the billing period changes, but it never writes to an ARIA live region. Screen‑reader users do not hear the discount information.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live region (e.g., `
`) and update its content inside **_updateSavingsMessage**, or call `liveRegion.announce(message);`. ---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not provide a live‑region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The function **setPeriod** updates the displayed prices on all plan cards, but it only announces the period change (`liveRegion.announce`). The actual price numbers that change are not announced, so users cannot know the new amounts.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating each plan’s price, call `liveRegion.announce(`Price for ${plan.id} is now ${priceFormatter.format(newPrice)}`);` or update a dedicated live region that reflects the current price for the highlighted plan.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison‑table toggle button lacks **aria-controls** on init
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison‑table “Show comparison” button is wired with `setExpanded` when opened, but on page load the button does **not** have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the table’s ID. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it reveals.
issuesFound.fix:
In the init routine, after locating the toggle button (`_toggleEl`), add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', _comparisonTable.id);` where `_comparisonTable` is the element with the comparison data.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison‑table toggle button lacks initial **aria‑expanded** state
issuesFound.explanation: The same comparison‑table toggle button does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load, violating the requirement that disclosure controls expose their collapsed state initially.
issuesFound.fix:
During init, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` so the collapsed state is correctly exposed before any interaction.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle does not announce mode changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The **_onViewToggle** handler switches between grid and list view and updates CSS classes, but it never announces the new view mode to screen readers. Users cannot know whether the layout has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the view mode, call `liveRegion.announce(`View changed to ${mode} view`);` or update a dedicated live region element with that message.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter “Reset filters” action does not announce the reset to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The **resetFilters** method restores default filter values and updates the UI, but it does not provide any live‑region feedback. Screen‑reader users receive no indication that the filters have been cleared.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce('All filters have been reset');` at the end of **resetFilters**, or update a hidden status element with the same message.

---

issuesFound.title: Pagination changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The **_syncPagination** function updates the pagination text (e.g., “Page 2 of 5”) but does not announce the new page number. Users of assistive technologies cannot tell which page of results is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the pagination text, call `liveRegion.announce(`Page ${current} of ${pages}`);` or update a live‑region element dedicated to pagination status.

---

issuesFound.title: Sorting changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The **_onSortChange** handler updates the sort order of the product grid, but it does not provide any live‑region announcement of the new sort criteria. Screen‑reader users are unaware that the ordering has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new sort, call `liveRegion.announce(`Sorted by ${s.sortKey} ${s.sortDir === 'desc' ? 'descending' : 'ascending'}`);` or update a hidden status element with that message.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to “true”
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the drawer UI but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true". Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() right after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but it never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element. Assistive technologies may still expose the hidden drawer to users.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the drawer, call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (and remove it when opening).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button. The button therefore does not convey its expanded state to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Inside _openInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the panel is opened.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close routine does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes the open class but never resets aria‑expanded to "false" on the trigger button, leaving the state stale for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Inside _closeInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when the panel is closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code sets the hidden attribute but does not also set aria‑hidden="true". Some AT ignore the hidden attribute alone.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance() after panel.setAttribute('hidden','') add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discountCodeInput module validates the entered code and shows a status message, but it never marks the input with aria‑invalid when the code is invalid. Users of assistive tech receive no programmatic error indication.
issuesFound.fix: In _onApply() when result.valid is false, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input validation does not clear aria‑invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: After a successful discount code entry the input remains with aria‑invalid if it was previously set, because the code never removes the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of _onApply() add removeAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid'); to clear the error state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible UI but no aria‑live region is updated, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance() and _closeInstance() call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened') or ('Menu closed') or update a dedicated status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay click does not set aria‑hidden on overlay when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav._onOutsideClick() closes the drawer but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the overlay element, leaving it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _close() after hiding the overlay, add setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing `aria‑expanded` on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button is given `role="button"` and `aria-controls`, but it never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute when the page loads. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the associated answer panel is collapsed or expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the `faqRegistry.parse` function after the button is identified.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set `aria‑expanded` to `true`
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, announceToSR)` function adds the CSS class to show the panel and removes the `hidden` attribute, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state to `"true"`. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `open`, after `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button missing `aria‑controls` linking to the pricing panel
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected by `SELECTORS.pricingToggle` (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` and `aria‑checked`, but it never receives an `aria‑controls` attribute that points to the region it toggles (the pricing cards). Assistive technologies cannot associate the switch with the content it controls.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing.init`, after locating `_toggleEl`, add `if (_toggleEl && _gridEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id || srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'pricing-grid'));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer trigger may lack `aria‑controls` when drawer has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init` the trigger button receives `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`) does not have an `id` in the markup, the button ends up without `aria‑controls`, breaking the relationship between the button and the drawer for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element always has an `id` (e.g., add `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer');`) before setting `aria-controls` on the trigger.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not clear `aria‑expanded` on the trigger when the panel is hidden via keyboard (Escape)
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item, announceToSR)` function removes the `open` class and hides the panel, but it only sets `aria‑expanded` to `"false"` when the close is triggered by a click. When the panel is closed via the Escape key (handled in `_onTriggerKeydown`), the `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated, leaving the state inaccurate.
issuesFound.fix:
In the Escape‑key handling branch of `_onTriggerKeydown`, after calling `close(item, true)`, also call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (or ensure `close` always sets it, which it already does; just make sure the Escape path reaches `close`).  

These five issues directly correspond to elements and functions identified in the source code and satisfy the required reporting format.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate (lines 4175‑4192) calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds an error message element; it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); before marking the field invalid.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same inlineValidator.validate function removes the error message but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: When validation passes, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: close (lines 2265‑2278) collapses the panel with animation but never updates the trigger’s aria-expanded attribute, so the button’s state remains “true” after the answer is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: open (lines 2250‑2263) expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving its state ambiguous to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls reference to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init (lines 1045‑1055) the code only adds aria-controls to the trigger if the drawer element already has an id. The drawer markup does not include an id, so the button lacks an aria‑controls link to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has a unique id (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") and set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id); unconditionally.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu hide routine does not set aria‑hidden on the panel
Mobile navigation drawer close routine does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer
Inline field validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls reference to the drawer

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never updates `aria-pressed`. This violates the requirement for toggle buttons to reflect their state.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-expanded`, also call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger does not reference the drawer it controls via `aria-controls`, so assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has an `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`) and add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer')` during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the visibility of the panel is toggled, but the corresponding trigger button never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; inside `_closeInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not set aria‑expanded when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands the answer panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button, while `close(item)` correctly sets it to `"false"`. This leaves the button’s state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of `open(item)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items lack initial aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the FAQ page loads, the trigger buttons are given `role="button"` and `aria-controls`, but no `aria-expanded` attribute is set (neither `"true"` nor `"false"`). AT therefore cannot infer the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` unless the item is marked open.

issuesFound.title: `setExpanded()` utility hides panels without setting aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The helper adds the `hidden` attribute to the target element but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"`. Elements hidden only with `hidden` may still be exposed to some assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form does not set aria‑invalid on an invalid email field
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onSubmit()` handler validates the email address manually with `validateField`, but when the validation fails it only displays a status message and does not apply `aria-invalid="true"` to the `<input>` element. Users of AT will not be notified of the error.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the billing‑period buttons updates the displayed prices, but no `aria-live` region is updated to announce “Monthly billing selected” or “Annual billing selected”. Screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After `setPeriod(period, ...)` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) || \`Billing period set to ${period}\`)`.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button does not announce the new layout
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view updates CSS classes but does not provide an audible cue. AT users are unaware that the layout changed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the view mode, update a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('View mode switched to list')`).

issuesFound.title: Autocomplete suggestion list does not use a live region for updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.render()` function populates the listbox but does not set `aria-live` on the container or announce the number of suggestions. AT users may not be aware that suggestions appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the listbox (`[role="listbox"]`) has `aria-live="polite"` (already set) and after rendering call `liveRegion.announce(\`\${suggestions.length} suggestions available\`)`.

fpTitles: Autocomplete suggestion list does not use a live region for updates

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions add/remove CSS classes and hide/show the navigation drawer, but they never call setAttribute('aria‑expanded', …) on the hamburger button that triggers the drawer. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is a toggle control that opens and closes the mobile navigation, yet it never receives an aria‑pressed state. Assistive technologies cannot convey its current state.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating aria‑expanded also set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false');  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls attribute (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the mobile drawer does not reference the drawer element via aria‑controls, so the relationship between control and controlled region is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., nav-drawer) and add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer'); during initialization.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on dropdown menu trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance the code adds/removes CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the panel, but never updates the aria‑expanded state on the button that controls the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on FAQ accordion items
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions animate the answer panel but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, leaving AT unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (mobile navigation drawer)
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, the code adds the hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (dropdown menu panels)
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance removes the hidden attribute from the panel but never sets aria‑hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix:
After adding hidden to the panel, also call setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (FAQ panels)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel but does not clear aria‑hidden, and close adds hidden without setting aria‑hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix:
In open add removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden'); and in close add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (product filter count badge)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge text is updated, but no aria‑live region is used to announce the new count to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, set its aria‑label and call liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (product filter empty‑state message)
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, the empty‑state element is shown/hidden without any live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware of the result.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an aria‑live="polite" element (or reuse the status region) and announce `No products match your filters.` when the empty state becomes visible.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on pricing period simple switch
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`_toggleEl`) updates aria‑checked but does not set aria‑expanded to reflect the expanded/collapsed state of the pricing table, which is a disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix:
In pricing._syncToggleState add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed on pricing period simple switch (optional)
issuesFound.explanation: The same billing‑period switch is a toggle control; using aria‑pressed instead of aria‑checked provides clearer state for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Replace setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', …) with setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', …) or add both.

fpTitles: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (dropdown menu panels)
Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (FAQ panels)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav – toggle open does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes to show the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger trigger (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` right after the drawer is opened (inside `open()`).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav – toggle close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer without resetting `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the attribute stuck on “true”.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` before the drawer is hidden (inside `close()`).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav – trigger missing aria‑controls on init
issuesFound.explanation: During `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button is never given an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with its controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer element, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav – overlay hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the overlay is hidden (`_close()`), the code only removes the CSS class and sets `hidden` on the element, but never adds `aria-hidden="true"`. The overlay remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the overlay, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (and remove it when shown).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu – open instance does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` makes a dropdown panel visible but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu – close instance does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel but does not reset `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the state stale for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu – panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden by adding a CSS class and the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is never applied, so the panel stays perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the panel, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it when shown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion – close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `close(item)` collapses the answer without resetting `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the attribute incorrect.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing the FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion – panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer is collapsed, the panel is hidden via `hidden` attribute but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`, so it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the panel, call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (and remove it when shown).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate – does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The core validation routine returns a result but never applies `aria-invalid="true"` to the field when validation fails; only CSS classes are toggled. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive error state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `validateField` (or after calling it), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` on failure and remove the attribute on success.

issuesFound.title: productFilter – product count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the visible count but does not announce the new total to screen readers, leaving users unaware of filtered results.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, also update a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`).

issuesFound.title: pricing – billing‑period toggle does not announce changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod()` updates UI and price values but never announces the new billing period (e.g., “Annual billing selected”) to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard – step navigation does not announce the new step via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_onPrev()` and `_onNext()` change the visible step but do not provide an ARIA live announcement, so screen‑reader users cannot track progress.
issuesFound.fix: After moving to a new step, call `liveRegion.announce(`Step ${_current+1} of ${_steps.length}: ${_steps[_current].title}`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav – aria‑expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button starts without an `aria-expanded` attribute; the attribute is only set after the first interaction, violating WCAG 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()`, after locating `_trigger`, set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu – aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for some triggers
faqAccordion – aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for some items
dynamically hidden elements (mobileNav overlay, dropdown panels, FAQ panels) are not removed from the accessibility tree via aria‑hidden when hidden
mobileNav – trigger missing aria‑controls attribute (already Issue 3).
dropdownMenu – trigger missing aria‑controls attribute (handled in register; present). No issue.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `.mobileNav.toggle()` function toggles the mobile drawer but never calls `setAttribute('aria-expanded', …)` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true')`; in `close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger (`_trigger`) has no `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element, so assistive tech cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining `_drawer`, ensure `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not updated with aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `close()` removes CSS classes but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on `_drawer`; `open()` does not remove `aria-hidden`. This leaves the drawer in the accessibility tree even when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')`; in `open()` add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN)`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` toggle visibility of the panel but never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger element, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel not updated with aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code only removes CSS classes; it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, so the hidden content remains perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')`; in `_openInstance` add `removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item)` and `close(item)` toggle the panel but never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel not updated with aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; `aria-hidden` is never set, leaving the hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')`; in `open(item)` add `removeAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive a visual “active” class but never set `aria-pressed` to reflect their pressed state, making the toggle ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState` add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btnPeriod === period ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides on scroll, the code adds the `headerHidden` class but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the header element, leaving it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onScroll` when `_hidden` becomes true, add `setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')`; when it becomes false, `removeAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN)`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` updates the visible count but does not announce the change to screen readers, leaving users unaware of result count changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod` updates UI and prices but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users are not informed when the billing period switches.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new period, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to '+BILLING_PERIODS[period].label)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item)` and `close(item)` change visibility but never announce the state change, violating the requirement for dynamic content updates.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded')`; in `close(item)` add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `open()` and `close()` toggle the drawer without announcing the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened')`; in `close()` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` change panel visibility but do not announce the state change.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; in `_closeInstance` add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions do not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validation logic adds error messages and CSS classes but never calls `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when a field fails validation, breaking the ARIA requirement.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate` after detecting an invalid result, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true')`; on successful validation, `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID)`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes error classes but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the same validation routine, after a successful validation, call `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID)`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle functions that hide elements do not set aria‑hidden on the hidden element
issuesFound.explanation: `close()` hides the drawer via CSS but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')` in `close()`; add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN)` in `open()`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels do not have aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by removing a CSS class but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')` in `_closeInstance`; remove it in `_openInstance`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels do not have aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; `aria-hidden` is never set, leaving hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')` in `close`; remove it in `open`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and its open/close helpers) add/remove CSS classes to show/hide the drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` respectively (or toggle the attribute in toggle()).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance only add/remove CSS classes and hidden attributes on the panel. They never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the state invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion panel does not update aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open and _close show/hide the suggestion list but never set aria‑expanded on the search <input>. Users of screen readers cannot tell whether the autocomplete is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In _open add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in _close add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In _toggleClick (and the keyboard handler _onToggleKeydown) call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') !== 'true'))`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not update aria‑expanded on their triggers
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close add/remove CSS classes and animate height, but never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`[data-faq-trigger]`). Screen‑reader users cannot know which FAQ is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in close add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the code removes the “open” class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden without aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by adding a CSS class and setting hidden, but `aria-hidden` is never applied, so hidden content remains perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding, call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; after showing, call `removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list is hidden without aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: The list is hidden by adding the hidden attribute only; `aria-hidden` is not set, so screen readers may still read the list items.
issuesFound.fix:
In hide() add `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in show() remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields**
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code adds an error message element but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
In inlineValidator.validate after a failed result call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields**
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously‑invalid field becomes valid the code removes the error message but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the invalid state lingering for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After a successful validation call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Open all / Close all” buttons lack aria‑controls**
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons trigger opening/closing of all FAQ items but do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the FAQ list, nor do they update `aria-expanded` on the individual triggers, making the relationship unclear.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(openAllBtn, 'aria-controls', _containerEl.id)` (and similarly for closeAllBtn) and ensure each FAQ item’s trigger receives the correct `aria-expanded` state (already fixed in Issue 5).

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons (`data-period`) lack aria‑pressed**
issuesFound.explanation: Period buttons are styled as a tab list but never receive `aria-pressed` or `role="tab"` attributes, breaking the ARIA tab pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In _setToggleState add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` and ensure the container has `role="tablist"` and each button has `role="tab"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region announcements for dynamic content are missing for several UI actions**
issuesFound.explanation: Users of screen readers receive no audible feedback that search results have changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting _statusEl.textContent, add `liveRegion.announce(_statusEl.textContent)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Wizard step navigation does not announce step changes via a live region**
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the step container (or a dedicated element) has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` or use liveRegion.announceAssertive if immediate.

---

These issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and the additional constraints.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion panels do not update aria‑expanded on their triggers
Dropdown panels are hidden without aria‑hidden**

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `mobileNav.init()` and update it to `"true"`/`"false"` in the `open()` and `close()` functions (lines 1544‑1556).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The same toggle button does not have an `aria‑controls` attribute that points to the drawer element, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining the drawer element (`_drawer`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-drawer'))` in `mobileNav.init()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`close()`), the code only removes CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer, leaving it in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open dropdown panels never receive an `aria‑expanded` state, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance`, after setting the panel visible, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance`, set it to `"false"`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons receive `aria-controls` but never get an `aria-expanded` attribute, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false')`; update this attribute in `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the number of visible products changes, the UI updates a badge but does not announce the new count to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the result of their filter.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices but provides no live‑region announcement, so users of assistive tech are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Billing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item updates the DOM but does not provide an audible cue via a live region, making the change invisible to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()`, after setting `item.open = true`, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`)`; similarly in `close()`, announce “collapsed”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑controls (fallback)
issuesFound.explanation: Although the code attempts to set `aria-controls` if missing, the `panelId` is generated only when the panel lacks an id; if the panel already has an id, the attribute may be omitted, breaking the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` is always executed, regardless of existing id, by moving the call outside the conditional.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS but not removed from accessibility tree on close
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden with CSS classes but remains focusable and announced by screen readers because `aria-hidden` is never set.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after adding the hidden class, also call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `role="region"` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels receive `role="region"` only when a role is not already set, but when hidden they may lose the role, reducing landmark consistency.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel always has `role="region"` and toggle `hidden` attribute without removing the role.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not have `aria‑pressed` to reflect open state
issuesFound.explanation: The button’s visual state changes, but assistive technologies cannot detect it via `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `close()` set to `"false"`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code removes the open class but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`, leaving the hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` in `_closeInstance`; remove it in `_openInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter hidden cards are not marked with `aria‑hidden` until after animation completes
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria‑hidden` attribute is applied only after the fade‑out animation, causing a brief period where hidden content is still perceivable.
issuesFound.fix: Set `aria-hidden="true"` immediately when starting the hide animation, and remove it when showing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks `aria‑pressed` state for screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle switch changes visual state but does not expose its state via `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick`, after toggling the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)`, set to `"false"`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop is a clickable <div> without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria‑hidden="true" onClick={onClose} />` is interactive (onClick) but lacks `role="button"` and a `tabIndex`, making it inaccessible to keyboard and screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria‑hidden="true" onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay is a clickable <div> without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` handles click events to close the dialog but does not expose an accessible role or keyboard handling, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 938: `<div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription button uses wrong prop name, so loading state is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `<Button … loading={isSubmitting} …>` passes a non‑existent `loading` prop; the `Button` component expects `isLoading`. Consequently `aria‑busy` is never set and AT does not know the button is loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 714: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button uses wrong prop name, so loading state is not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: `<Button … loading={isSubmitting} …>` passes `loading` instead of the required `isLoading`, so `aria‑busy` is omitted and the loading state is invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field references a non‑existent error element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: `<TextInput id="contact-name" … aria-describedby={...}>` includes `contact-name-error` in `aria-describedby`, but no element with that id is rendered, breaking the reference and leaving users without the error context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: remove the broken reference – change to `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” field references a non‑existent error element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: `<TextInput id="contact-email" … aria-describedby={...}>` includes `contact-email-error` but no element with that id exists, breaking the ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: remove the broken reference – change to `<TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” field references a non‑existent error element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: `<TextInput id="contact-subject" … aria-describedby={...}>` includes `contact-subject-error` but no matching element is rendered, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: remove the broken reference – change to `<TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea references a non‑existent error element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: `<Textarea id="contact-message" … aria-describedby={...}>` includes `contact-message-error` (and the counter id) but no element with `contact-message-error` exists, breaking the ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1127: remove the broken error reference while keeping the counter – change to `<Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={counterId} />`

issuesFound.title: Loading state not announced on newsletter and contact form submit buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> components use a non‑standard loading prop instead of the expected isLoading prop, so aria‑busy is never set. Assistive technologies cannot detect the loading state.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace loading={isSubmitting} with isLoading={isSubmitting} (and keep aria-disabled if desired).

  Line 262: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: Email input error message not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id exists, so screen readers cannot associate the error message with the field. Affects users submitting the newsletter form.

Add an id to the error message component and reference it, e.g.:

issuesFound.title: Contact form field errors not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: Each TextInput in the contact form references an aria-describedby ID (e.g., “contact-name-error”) that does not exist because the error messages are rendered only in the summary, not inline. AT users cannot hear field‑specific errors. Affects all contact‑form users.
issuesFound.fix:
For each field, render an ErrorMessage with the matching id, e.g.:  
  Line 1510: `<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} />`  
  Repeat for email, subject, and message fields (ids contact-email-error, contact-subject-error, contact-message-error).

```tsx
  isLoading={isSubmitting}
  aria-busy={isSubmitting}
  aria-describedby={statusId}
>
```

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size and link underline removed: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px (below the recommended 44px) and remove underlines without a non-colour hover indicator (only colour changes on hover). This affects touch accuracy and link identification for users with low vision (WCAG 1.4.1, 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector removes underlines and relies solely on colour change for hover states. WCAG 1.4.1 requires a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border, or background change) to indicate link status for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule-block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements in standard mode, blocking keyboard navigation visibility (WCAG 2.4.7).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and only adds outline-offset, which renders nothing when outline is none. This removes the focus ring for buttons in standard mode, failing WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from layout
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This clutters the visual interface with labels intended to be hidden (WCAG 1.3.1).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition to slide into view, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 733 is empty and does not disable this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.fix: Line 569: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's focus-visible state sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus indicator specifically for keyboard navigation on the search field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 609: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none with only an offset. This removes the visible focus ring, making the button difficult to identify for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 44px for touch targets. This makes buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the absolute minimum of 24px and far below the recommended 44px. This makes small buttons extremely difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update `aria-expanded` on the trigger, but they do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1642: liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');
  Line 1657: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module removes the open class from `_drawer` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element itself. If the CSS does not apply `display: none`, the drawer remains accessible to assistive technologies even when visually closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visibility change not reflected in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function in the `stickyHeader` module toggles the `headerHidden` class to hide the header but does not update the `aria-hidden` attribute. Screen readers may still navigate to header content that is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1505: setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, _hidden ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal elements not managed for accessibility during animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `_reveal` function in the `viewportReveal` module adds a visible class to elements but does not remove `aria-hidden` if the elements were initially hidden. Content may remain hidden from assistive technologies even after it becomes visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2665: el.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner appearance not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in the `cookieConsent` module displays the consent dialog and traps focus but does not announce the banner's appearance to a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware the dialog has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner displayed. Please review our cookie policy.');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic content update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in the `statCounterAnimator` module updates the text content of number counters dynamically but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen readers will not detect the counting animation or the final value update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2745: liveRegion.announce(`Stat updated to ${_format(cfg.target, cfg)}`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function in the `scrollToTop` module shows or hides the button based on scroll position but does not announce the visibility change. Users may not know the button has become available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1565: liveRegion.announce(shouldShow ? 'Scroll to top button visible' : 'Scroll to top button hidden');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button uses incorrect ARIA state attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function in the `pricing` module sets `aria-checked` on `_toggleEl`. If `_toggleEl` is a `<button>` (which retains its native role if not explicitly changed to 'switch'), it should use `aria-pressed` instead of `aria-checked` to indicate toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_addFile` function in the `fileUploadField` module validates files and announces errors via live region if validation fails, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `inputEl`. Form validation errors must be programmatically associated with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in the `discountCodeInput` module validates the discount code and displays an error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on `_inputEl` when the code is invalid. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons aria-pressed not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function loads the user's preference from storage and applies the theme, but it does not update the aria-pressed state on the [data-theme-toggle] buttons to match. This leaves the button states out of sync with the actual theme until the user interacts with them.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2730: In themeSwitcher.init, after setting _current = _load(), call set(_current) or manually update button states: qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)); });

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update aria-expanded on triggers, but they do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1430: In dropdownMenu._openInstance, add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');. Line ~1450: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discountCodeInput._onApply function validates the input and displays an error message in _statusEl if the code is invalid, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the _inputEl itself. Assistive technologies may not identify the input as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2500: In discountCodeInput._onApply, inside the if (!result.valid) block, add: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');. Also ensure it is removed on success.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visually hides header without updating aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader._onScroll function adds the CSS.headerHidden class to hide the header visually when scrolling down, but it does not set aria-hidden="true" on the header element. If the CSS does not use display: none, the header remains in the accessibility tree despite being invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1600: In stickyHeader._onScroll, when adding CSS.headerHidden, also add: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');. When removing the class, set: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised if autoPlay is false
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function only calls play() (which sets aria-pressed="true") if autoPlay is enabled. If autoPlay is disabled, neither play() nor pause() is called, leaving the _playBtn without an initial aria-pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2950: In testimonialsCarousel.init, after the autoPlay check, ensure state is set: if (!_playing) { pause(); } or explicitly setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); if autoPlay is off.

issuesFound.title: pricing card updates not announced via aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._updatePlanCard function updates the text content of priceDisplay, pricePeriod, and other elements when the billing period changes, but these elements do not have aria-live attributes. Screen reader users are not announced the new price values, only the period change message.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2200: In pricing.init or planDataParser.parse, add aria-live="polite" to the price display elements (e.g., plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the mobile navigation drawer but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed. This leaves users unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not set aria-hidden on dynamically hidden elements
issuesFound.explanation: The close function sets the hidden attribute on the overlay and removes classes from the drawer, but it does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true" on these elements. This may leave them accessible to assistive technologies despite being visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The trigger remains in a collapsed state for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not announce the closure via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not set aria-hidden on dynamically hidden elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute on the panel after a delay but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". This risks the panel remaining in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. Assistive technologies will incorrectly report the answer as collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users are not informed that the answer has been hidden.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not set aria-hidden on dynamically hidden elements
issuesFound.explanation: The close function sets the hidden attribute on the panel via the animation callback but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". The panel may remain accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded on interaction

Line ~2835: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
