issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle the CSS class and visibility of the drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.open, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (_trigger) does not have aria-expanded="false" set during initialisation. Assistive technologies will not know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.init, after _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle), add if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close instance functions manage the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed when the dropdown state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In dropdownMenu._openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state and shows the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In faqAccordion.open, after item.open = true, add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In srUtils.markInvalid, add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); before addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: Validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. Fields may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In srUtils.markValid, add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); or field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Product count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters change, but the element lacks aria-live and no announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In productFilter.init, ensure _countBadge has aria-live="polite". In _updateCountBadge, add liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The price display text is updated dynamically when the billing period changes, but the element lacks aria-live and no announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the new price.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In pricing._updatePlanCard, ensure plan.priceDisplay has aria-live="polite". Or add liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`); after updating textContent.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat number is animated and updated dynamically, but the element lacks aria-live. Screen reader users will not hear the final count or the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In statCounterAnimator.init, add setAttr(el, 'aria-live', 'polite'); to observed elements. Or add liveRegion.announce(_format(cfg.target, cfg)); in onComplete.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened or closed, no announcement is made to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In faqAccordion.open, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));. In faqAccordion.close, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button click not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll to top button is clicked, the page scrolls but no announcement is made. While focus moves, an explicit confirmation improves usability.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In scrollToTop._onClick, after scrollToY, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner not hidden from accessibility tree on close
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie banner is faded out and removed, but aria-hidden is not set to "true" before the transition. Screen readers may still access the banner during the fade animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In cookieConsent._hideBanner, before fadeOut, add setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner not hidden on load if consent already given
issuesFound.explanation: If the user has already given consent, the banner remains in the DOM without being hidden from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden or hidden attribute).
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In cookieConsent.init, if decision === ACCEPTED or DECLINED and _bannerEl exists, call _hideBanner() or setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Search input aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially. It is only set when the list is shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In suggestionListRenderer.mount, after wiring input, add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel aria-hidden not synchronized with visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel (inst.panel) is hidden using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated. This risks the panel remaining accessible to assistive technologies when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Module dropdownMenu, function _openInstance: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line Module dropdownMenu, function _closeInstance: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger), though the close() function correctly sets it to "false". This leaves the state inconsistent when an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module faqAccordion, function open: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel aria-hidden not synchronized with visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ answer panel (item.panel) visibility is toggled using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated. This may expose hidden content to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Module faqAccordion, function open: item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line Module faqAccordion, function close: item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper function associates an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module srUtils, function markInvalid: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid helper function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module srUtils, function markValid: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters change, but there is no accompanying announcement to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module productFilter, function _updateCountBadge: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab click action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab updates the product grid but does not trigger a live region announcement confirming the filter change. Users may not realize the content has been filtered.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module productFilter, function _onTabClick: liveRegion.announce(`Filter applied: ${value}`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the billing period updates prices dynamically but does not announce the change to a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware the prices have switched to monthly or annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module pricing, function setPeriod: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visibility change not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The header is visually hidden via CSS class when scrolling down, but aria-hidden is not updated. The header landmark may remain in the accessibility tree despite being invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module stickyHeader, function _onScroll: if (_hidden) _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); else _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (_inputEl) acts as a combobox trigger but does not have aria-expanded="false" set during initialization. The initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line Module siteSearch, function init: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the panel but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets item.open = true and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating a state mismatch.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _syncTabAriaStates() function updates the visual active state of filter tabs but omits the aria-pressed attribute update. This violates the toggle button pattern.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() helper links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() helper removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to false). The field remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader dynamically hidden without aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader hide() and show() functions toggle CSS classes to visually hide the header but do not update aria-hidden. The header remains in the accessibility tree even when invisible.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader component missing props for Demo Modal trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The SiteHeader component definition does not accept the onRequestDemo or demoTriggerRef props passed by LuminaryPage. This breaks the functionality to open the Demo Modal, making the feature inaccessible to users who expect a trigger in the header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1037: export interface SiteHeaderProps { items?: NavItem[]; onRequestDemo?: () => void; demoTriggerRef?: RefObject<HTMLButtonElement>; }

issuesFound.title: LuminaryPage passing unsupported props to SiteHeader
issuesFound.explanation: LuminaryPage passes onRequestDemo and demoTriggerRef to SiteHeader, but SiteHeader does not accept or use them. The Demo Modal remains unreachable, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as the functionality is effectively missing from the UI.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2247: <SiteHeader items={items} onRequestDemo={openDemoModal} demoTriggerRef={demoTriggerRef} /> (Ensure SiteHeader is updated to accept and implement these props, e.g., by adding a "Request Demo" button).

issuesFound.title: SearchInput form has redundant aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The form element uses aria-labelledby pointing to a visible label, but the input inside also has an aria-label. While not a failure, this creates potential redundancy where screen readers might announce conflicting or duplicate names for the form region versus the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1075: <form id="site-search" role="search" className="site-search-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}> (Remove aria-labelledby from form if the input's aria-label is sufficient, or ensure they are consistent).

issuesFound.title: SearchInput TextInput has redundant aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has an aria-label="Search" while also being associated with a visible <label> element via htmlFor. This provides two accessible names for the same input, which can be confusing for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1083: <TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" className="site-search-form__input" /> (Remove aria-label to rely on the associated <label>).

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Error Summary uses incorrect heading level
issuesFound.explanation: The Error Summary heading is an <h2>, but it is nested inside the ContactForm section which already has an <h2> (contact-heading). This skips a heading level (h2 -> h2), disrupting the document outline for users navigating by headings.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1969: <h3 id="contact-error-summary-heading" className="error-summary__heading"> (Change h2 to h3 to maintain hierarchy).

issuesFound.title: Spinner component has redundant accessible name and live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The Spinner component has role="status" (which implies aria-live="polite") but also explicitly sets aria-live="polite". Additionally, it has an aria-label and a VisuallyHidden child with the same text, causing potential double announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709: <span role="status" aria-label={label} className={`spinner spinner--${size}`}> (Remove aria-live="polite" as it is implied by role="status". Ensure VisuallyHidden text is not redundant with aria-label).

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component for the email field includes an aria-describedby attribute pointing to 'newsletter-email-error'. However, the parent FormGroup (Line 1745) does not pass the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage component with that ID is never rendered. Screen readers will announce a reference to an element that does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1745: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
  Line 1755: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" ... aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. Content remains visible in the layout while the class name suggests it should be screen-reader only.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative

issuesFound.title: Form label hidden from assistive technologies

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus because outline-offset alone does not render any visible indicator when outline-width is zero.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline and provides only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. Users relying on forced colors cannot see which element has keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input focus-visible state removes the default outline and sets outline-offset to 0 without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, leaving keyboard users with no visible focus state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible state removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus ring for keyboard users navigating to the button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible and only provide outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width and min-height to 20px, creating a touch target of only 20×20 pixels, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.2.

issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width and height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height to 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for optimal touch accessibility, potentially causing activation errors for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height to 36px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation links and buttons set min-height to 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.

issuesFound.explanation: The submenu links set min-height to 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for dropdown menu items.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height to 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width and height to 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for touch activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text remains visible to sighted users, and the utility does not function as intended.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation for users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition that persists in the prefers-reduced-motion media query, which currently contains only empty rules, potentially triggering motion sickness.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card has a transform transition that is not disabled in the reduced-motion media query, which only overrides the hover state transform property but leaves the transition active.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on card link arrows
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link arrow has a transform transition that is not disabled in the reduced-motion media query, potentially causing unwanted motion for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on stat items
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item has a transform transition that is not fully disabled in the reduced-motion media query, which only overrides the hover state but not the transition itself.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on pricing cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card has a transform transition that is not fully disabled in the reduced-motion media query, which only overrides the hover state transform.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This removes the default browser focus indicator without providing a replacement, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. Box-shadow is already suppressed in forced-colors mode, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349-351: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px as a replacement. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users without any indication of focus.

issuesFound.fix: Line 368: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This makes it impossible for sighted keyboard users to determine which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query overrides :focus-visible to remove the outline entirely, leaving no focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. Users with forced colors enabled cannot see which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes the open CSS class from the drawer element but does not set aria-hidden="true" or the hidden attribute. If the CSS does not use display:none or visibility:hidden when the class is removed, the drawer remains visible to screen readers even when visually closed. This causes screen reader users to encounter hidden navigation items when navigating the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1754-1774: Add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); immediately after setting _open = false; and remove the attribute in the open() function with removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-controls on the trigger pointing to the panel, and sets aria-expanded state, but does not set aria-labelledby on the panel element to reference the trigger. Screen reader users navigating the panel will not hear the context of what the panel is associated with.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1924-1927: After setting panelId and triggerId (using srUtils.ensureId), add setAttr(panel, 'aria-labelledby', triggerId); to explicitly label the panel with its trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons use conflicting ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons have both aria-pressed and aria-selected attributes set to the same value. These attributes have different semantic meanings: aria-pressed is for toggle buttons while aria-selected is for selectable items in lists, grids, or tablists. Having both creates conflicting information for assistive technologies about the control's behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2844-2847: Remove setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); and keep only setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));, or alternatively change the buttons to role="radio" in a role="radiogroup" and use only aria-selected.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer visually by removing CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. Screen readers can still access the drawer content via landmark navigation or reading modes even when it is visually closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not revealed to accessibility tree when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the drawer visually but does not remove the aria-hidden attribute that was set when it was closed. This prevents screen reader users from accessing the drawer content when it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function attempts to set aria-controls on the trigger button only if the drawer element already has an id attribute. It does not ensure the drawer has an id using ensureId(), so aria-controls may not be set if the HTML lacks an id, breaking the programmatic relationship between the trigger and the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: Replace `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls') && _drawer.id)` with `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) { const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel and updates aria-expanded, but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change. Users relying on screen readers may not be notified that the submenu has opened, especially if they are using touch or voice input methods that don't trigger focus moves.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1985: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Submenu opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel and updates aria-expanded, but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change. Users may not be aware that the menu has closed, particularly if focus is not returned to the trigger (when returnFocus is false).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Submenu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (_drawer) is shown and hidden by toggling CSS classes (is-open/nav-open) on the element. When closed, the JavaScript does not set aria-hidden="true" (or remove the element from the DOM). If the accompanying CSS uses visibility:hidden, opacity:0, or off-screen positioning instead of display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be navigated to by screen reader users even when visually closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2154: Add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') after removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open)
  Line 2126: Add removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden') after addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open)

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs use incorrect ARIA attribute for mutually exclusive selection
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons use aria-pressed to indicate the active filter state. aria-pressed is intended for toggle buttons that can be independently turned on or off (like a play/pause button). These filter tabs are mutually exclusive (only one can be active at a time, like radio buttons or tabs). Using aria-pressed causes screen readers to announce them as "toggle buttons" rather than selected options, which may confuse users about how the control behaves.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3366: Change setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) to setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)). Additionally, consider adding role="tab" to the buttons and role="tablist" to the container for better semantics, or use a native radio button group.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle uses conflicting ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual/Quarterly) have both aria-pressed and aria-selected attributes set simultaneously. These attributes have different meanings and should not be used together on the same element. aria-pressed indicates a toggle button state (on/off), while aria-selected indicates a selected item in a list (such as a tab). Using both creates ambiguous semantics for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Remove one of the attributes. For mutually exclusive period selection, remove Line 3720 (setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))) and keep only aria-selected, or better yet, implement these as role="tab" with aria-selected, or as native radio buttons.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel (`inst.panel`) and sets `inst.open = true`, but fails to update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. The `_closeInstance` function sets it to `"false"`, indicating the attribute is intended to be managed but the open path is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside `_openInstance`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets `item.open = true`, but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on `item.trigger`. The `close()` function sets `aria-expanded="false"`, so the toggle state is not communicated to assistive technologies when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function links the error message element via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as invalid. The companion `markValid` function removes `aria-invalid`, confirming the attribute is intended to be managed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `markInvalid` function, before or after the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles CSS classes on filter tab buttons (elements matching `[data-filter-value]`) to reflect the active category, but does not set `aria-pressed` to indicate the pressed state. Per the HTML contract comment in the code, these buttons act as toggle buttons and require `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800: Inside the `forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer and updates ARIA states, but does not announce the closed state to screen readers via the `liveRegion` utility. Users are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1870: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens a dropdown menu but does not announce the action via a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the dropdown content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` after the panel is shown (after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes a dropdown menu but does not announce the closed state via a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the dropdown has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` immediately before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function updates the panel visibility but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technology unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.explanation: The accordion open function animates the panel and updates classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.

issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline() missing live region announcement

issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the panel and updates state, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the disclosure pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2907: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Assistive technologies cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1127: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the `markInvalid` function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but does not announce the closure to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies receive no confirmation that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed, the function updates CSS and hides the panel but does not announce the collapsed state. Users are not informed that the menu has closed, especially when focus remains on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1444: Insert the following block before the closing brace of the `_closeInstance` function:
  ```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');
  ```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion close function collapses the panel and updates ARIA attributes but omits a live region announcement. Users do not receive audible confirmation that the answer has been collapsed.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded() missing collapse announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the comparison table is expanded, a live region announces the state. However, when collapsing the table (the else branch), no announcement is made, leaving screen reader users unaware that the table was hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2394: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.comparisonTableCollapsed || 'Comparison table collapsed');` immediately after the line `setExpanded(trigger, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate the active category via CSS classes but lack the aria-selected attribute. Since these buttons function as tabs (selecting one from many), they must expose the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2731: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` immediately after the line `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing comparison table trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The expand/collapse trigger for the comparison table is fetched from the DOM but is never given an aria-controls attribute pointing to the table element’s id. This prevents screen readers from identifying which element the button controls.

fpTitles: pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded() missing collapse announcement
pricing comparison table trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger elements
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). While the register() function sets an initial value, the state is never updated during interaction, leaving screen readers with stale information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2315 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands a FAQ panel and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). Although the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the asymmetric update leaves the attribute incorrect when items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520 (in open()): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function in productFilter toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not set the aria-pressed attribute to indicate the pressed state to screen readers. These buttons act as a toggle group where one is always active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in _syncTabAriaStates): Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() helper function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() helper function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Fields remain marked as invalid after they become valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490 (in markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function locates the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger lacks the necessary initial state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250 (in init() after _trigger is assigned): Add `if (_trigger && !_trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the result to screen readers. Users are not informed when the filter is applied or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050 (in applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays and toggle UI when switching between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380 (in setPeriod, after _syncToggleState): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` (or appropriate strings from STRINGS object).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands a FAQ panel but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users are not informed that content has been revealed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520 (in open()): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close() function collapses a FAQ panel but does not announce the action to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3560 (in close()): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function closes the navigation drawer but lacks the liveRegion.announce call present in the open() function. Users are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205 (in close()): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function reveals a dropdown panel but does not announce the state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function hides a dropdown panel but does not announce the state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2315 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before or after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer via CSS class removal but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element (_drawer). If CSS does not use display:none, the drawer remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205 (in close()): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Correspondingly, in open() at line 2180, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu toggle the 'open' boolean state and the hidden attribute on the panel, but they do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This violates SC 4.1.2 as the state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2936 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line ~2948 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function sets item.open = true and shows the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the state is never announced as opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3864 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid (srUtils.markInvalid)
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds the error message to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1184: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation valid state not clearing aria-invalid (srUtils.markValid)
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute) to indicate the field is now valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1200: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobileNav trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads and the menu is closed. This prevents screen readers from announcing the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2798 (in init function, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but does not have aria-hidden="true" applied. The content remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2828 (in close function): Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` after closing the drawer.
  Correspondingly in open function (Line ~2812): Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` or `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the function updates the visible cards and the count badge, but does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2529 (after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/yearly), the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realise the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3372 (after _updateSavingsMessage): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap created but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and deactivates it in the close() function, but the focus trap is never instantiated or activated in the open() function. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile menu when opened, allowing users to tab behind the modal drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers thinking the menu is still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after setting `_open = false;` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function sets inst.open = true and shows the panel, but never updates the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "true".
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown panel, the function sets inst.open = false and hides the panel, but never updates the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = true, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in the open function after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Collapses the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = false, but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the item.trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in the close function after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Associates an error message with the field via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in markInvalid before or after addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-selected on tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons are toggled visually via CSS classes, but neither aria-selected nor aria-pressed is updated to reflect the active tab state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop in _syncTabAriaStates.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion._onKeydown missing Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are non-button elements with role="button" (set in faqRegistry.parse), the keyboard handler only supports arrow navigation but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate the toggle, violating keyboard accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Add cases for `KEYS.ENTER` and `KEYS.SPACE` in _onKeydown to call `toggle(item)` when the trigger is focused.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Removes the error description from aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of markValid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: After selecting `_trigger`, add `if (!_trigger.hasAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element it controls, making the relationship unclear to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After ensuring _drawer has an id (using srUtils.ensureId), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the result count or that filtering has occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after the filter logic completes and before/after emit.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);` in setPeriod after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When activated, the page scrolls to top and focus moves, but the action is not announced via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of what happened.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` at the start of _onClick.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu visibility changes are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in _openInstance and `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ expand/collapse actions are not announced to screen readers, making it difficult for users to confirm their action.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` in open and `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` in close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and the 'hidden' attribute on the overlay, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot announce the current state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2852: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line 2870: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from knowing whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2958: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 2970: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in the siteSearch module manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but never set aria-expanded on the search input element (_inputEl). The input has role="combobox" and requires aria-expanded to communicate whether the suggestions are displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3805: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `if (!_searchState.open) return;` and before `if (!_inputEl.value) {`
  Line 3815: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `if (!_searchState.open) return;` at the start of the _close function

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (_toggleEl) in the pricing module cycles between monthly and annual billing states, but the _onToggleClick() handler and setPeriod() function do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate the active/pressed state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4210: Insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` at the end of the _onToggleClick function
  Line 4155: Insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` at the end of the setPeriod function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, no live region announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the state change. The STRINGS.announce.navOpen and navClosed messages exist but are not utilized in the open() and close() functions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2852: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
  Line 2870: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Users navigating with screen readers receive no confirmation that the menu has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2958: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2970: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates visually but no announcement is made via the live region to inform screen reader users of the pricing change or the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the toggle state in the setPeriod function

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in faqAccordion update the FAQ panel visibility and set aria-expanded on the trigger, but they do not announce the state change using the available STRINGS.announce.faqOpened and faqClosed messages via the live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4520: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 4545: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function in srUtils adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute from the field, leaving it marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2665: Insert `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after removing the describedby reference, or alternatively `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load when the navigation is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" when the dropdown panel is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2995: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function sets up the combobox pattern with role, aria-haspopup, and aria-controls, but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input element when the suggestion list is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3605: Insert `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer using CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the _drawer element or its container, leaving it in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens off-screen or behind them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2852: Insert `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: Insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` at the end of the close function before the final closing brace

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function accepts a returnFocus parameter but does not implement logic to return focus to the trigger element when closing the dropdown, violating the expected keyboard interaction pattern for menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2970: Insert `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` at the end of the _closeInstance function

issuesFound.title: contactForm first name missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input (SELECTORS.contactFirstName) is validated and bound but the code does not set the autocomplete="given-name" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill for personal data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5995: Insert `const firstNameField = qs(SELECTORS.contactFirstName, _formEl); if (firstNameField) firstNameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');` inside the init function after getting _formEl

issuesFound.title: contactForm last name missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form last name input (SELECTORS.contactLastName) lacks the autocomplete="family-name" attribute, preventing proper autofill functionality for users' personal data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5996: Insert `const lastNameField = qs(SELECTORS.contactLastName, _formEl); if (lastNameField) lastNameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');` inside the init function

issuesFound.title: contactForm email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input (SELECTORS.contactEmail) does not have autocomplete="email" set by the JavaScript, which is required for personal data inputs to support browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5997: Insert `const emailField = qs(SELECTORS.contactEmail, _formEl); if (emailField) emailField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` inside the init function

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input is initialized and validated but the code does not set autocomplete="email", which is required for email personal data fields to support browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5890: Insert `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` inside the init function after getting _emailEl

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step 1 name field missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form step 1 contains a name field (wizard_name) that is bound for validation but does not receive autocomplete="name", preventing autofill for this personal data field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6150: Insert `const nameField = qs('[name="wizard_name"]', step.panelEl); if (nameField) nameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` inside the step initialization loop where fields are bound

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step 1 email field missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form step 1 email field (wizard_email) is validated but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute required for personal data autofill support.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6151: Insert `const emailField = qs('[name="wizard_email"]', step.panelEl); if (emailField) emailField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` inside the step initialization loop

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when in error state, but FormGroup was not passed errorId or errorMessage props (it received error instead of errorMessage), so ErrorMessage is never rendered with that ID. Screen readers cannot announce the field validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when in error state, but FormGroup receives `error` prop instead of `errorMessage` and no `errorId`, so ErrorMessage is never rendered. Screen readers cannot announce the validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1733: Change `error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when in error state, but FormGroup is missing errorId and errorMessage props. The referenced ID does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1751: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when in error state, but FormGroup is missing errorId and errorMessage props. The referenced ID does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: Change `error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea has aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" when in error state, but FormGroup is missing errorId and errorMessage props. The error ID does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1787: Change `error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm ErrorMessage passed children instead of message prop
issuesFound.explanation: ErrorMessage component expects a `message` string prop, but children were passed instead. When status is error, the error message is not rendered because the component returns null when message is undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1668: Replace `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert">Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment.</ErrorMessage>` with `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm ErrorMessage passed children instead of message prop
issuesFound.explanation: ErrorMessage component expects a `message` string prop, but children were passed instead. The server error message is not rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1814: Replace `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" role="alert">Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again.</ErrorMessage>` with `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />`.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog div. Screen readers may not correctly identify the dialog as modal, allowing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1874: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the overlay div. Line 1878: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the dialog div with `role="dialog"`.

issuesFound.title: Button loading prop name mismatch in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component interface defines the loading state prop as `isLoading`, but NewsletterForm passes `loading={isSubmitting}`. This prevents the button from entering the loading state, breaking the accessibility requirement to communicate busy state via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1763: Change `loading={isSubmitting}` to `isLoading={isSubmitting}`

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-describedby reference in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The submit Button has `aria-describedby={statusId}` referencing "newsletter-status", but the element with that ID only renders when `status === 'success'`. When the form is visible (status !== 'success'), the referenced element does not exist, creating a broken reference for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1766: Remove `aria-describedby={statusId}` from the Button element

issuesFound.title: Invalid validation rule structure in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The useField hook expects ValidationRule objects with a `validate` function property, but the code passes objects with `fn` and `msg` properties. This prevents validation from functioning, causing form submission without proper validation feedback to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1724-1727: Replace with `{ validate: (v: string) => v.trim().length > 0 ? undefined : 'Email address is required.' }, { validate: (v: string) => /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(v) ? undefined : 'Enter a valid email address.' }`

issuesFound.title: Invalid validation rule structure in ContactForm
issuesFound.explanation: The useField hook expects ValidationRule objects with a `validate` function property, but the code passes objects with `fn` and `msg` properties. This prevents validation from functioning, causing form submission without proper validation feedback to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1820: Replace with `{ validate: (v: string) => v.trim().length > 0 ? undefined : 'Full name is required.' }`
  Fix: Line 1821-1824: Replace with `{ validate: (v: string) => v.trim().length > 0 ? undefined : 'Email address is required.' }, { validate: (v: string) => /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(v) ? undefined : 'Enter a valid email address.' }`
  Fix: Line 1825: Replace with `{ validate: (v: string) => v.trim().length > 0 ? undefined : 'Subject is required.' }`
  Fix: Line 1826-1829: Replace with `{ validate: (v: string) => v.trim().length > 0 ? undefined : 'Message is required.' }, { validate: (v: string) => v.length <= MSG_MAX ? undefined : `Message must be ${MSG_MAX} characters or fewer.` }`

issuesFound.title: aria-label on table data cells in ComparisonTableSection
issuesFound.explanation: The ComparisonCell component applies aria-label to `<td>` elements. aria-label is not a supported attribute on table cells according to the ARIA specification and is not reliably announced by assistive technologies. The checkmark and dash symbols alone may not convey sufficient information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1383: Replace `{value}` with `Yes{value}` Fix: Line 1386: Replace `{value}` with `Not included{value}`

issuesFound.title: aria-modal on wrong element in Modal component
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places `aria-modal="true"` on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. According to WAI-ARIA practices, aria-modal should be placed on the element with role="dialog" to indicate that the content beneath the dialog is inert.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1907: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the overlay div
  Fix: Line 1910: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the div with `role="dialog"`

issuesFound.title: Extraneous props passed to SiteHeader in LuminaryPage
issuesFound.explanation: The SiteHeader component is rendered with `onRequestDemo` and `demoTriggerRef` props, but the SiteHeader component interface only accepts `items?: NavItem[]`. These props are ignored, potentially breaking the focus management intended for the demo modal trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1972: Change `<SiteHeader onRequestDemo={openDemoModal} demoTriggerRef={demoTriggerRef} />` to `<SiteHeader />`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput in the newsletter form has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the document. Screen readers will fail to announce the error message when validation fails.

issuesFound.fix: Line 2149: Change `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input references non-existent error element
