issuesFound.fix: Line 2167: Change `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the subject field has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist. Screen readers cannot associate the error message with the input.

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea for the message field has aria-describedby="contact-message-error", but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist. Screen readers cannot associate the error message with the textarea.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop hidden from assistive technology while clickable
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop div has aria-hidden="true" but also has an onClick handler to close the menu. Interactive elements must not be hidden from assistive technology, as screen reader users will not perceive the clickable region and cannot activate it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Remove `aria-hidden="true"` from the backdrop div. The element should remain unfocusable (no tabIndex) but not hidden from the accessibility tree. `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId and errorMessage
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an `error` prop instead of `errorMessage`, and lacks the required `errorId` prop. Consequently, the error message is not rendered in the DOM, and the input's aria-describedby references an ID that does not exist, breaking the connection between the field and its error message for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>` change to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name FormGroup missing errorId and errorMessage
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup receives an `error` prop instead of `errorMessage` and lacks `errorId`, preventing the error message from rendering and causing the input's aria-describedby to reference a non-existent ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>` change to `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm server ErrorMessage not rendering
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop but receives children instead. Since the `message` prop is undefined, the component returns null and the error text is not rendered or announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert">Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment.</ErrorMessage>` change to `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm server ErrorMessage not rendering
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop but receives children instead. Since the `message` prop is undefined, the component returns null and the error text is not rendered or announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" role="alert">Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again.</ErrorMessage>` change to `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />`.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to `aria-invalid` on the underlying input element. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />` change to `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrect aria-modal placement
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is placed on the overlay element instead of the dialog element. This causes assistive technologies to potentially treat the overlay as the modal context rather than the actual dialog content, potentially allowing navigation outside the dialog when it should be trapped.
issuesFound.fix: Line with `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` remove `aria-modal="true"` from this line, and on the line with `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>` add `aria-modal="true"` so it becomes `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', \`modal-dialog--${size}\`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`.
